explored_directions.description: Stash 2.0 — A thought translator that distills fragmented AI prompts into time-compressed intent narratives, presented through an ambient pixel-art desktop companion
explored_directions.differentiator: The only product combining auto-distillation of AI coding prompts into layered intent narratives with an ambient desktop pet UI for peripheral awareness
explored_directions.risk: Linux transparent window behavior, LLM Chinese quality, terminal-specific jump APIs

decision_log.detail: User skipped Layer 1 direction exploration. Product spec was pre-defined by user — used directly as selected direction.
User explicitly requested ALL features F1-F9 be included. No MVP cutting performed. User stated: '不做MVP全部的feature都要实现'
User confirmed full feature scope including 3 high-complexity challenges (Linux window behavior, jump-to-terminal, LLM hybrid). User response: 'ACCEPT_COST'.
16 requirements (REQ-001 through REQ-016) and 7 invariants (INV-001 through INV-007) defined with EARS acceptance criteria. Privacy: no sanitization, encryption deferred.
UI Taste exploration completed via Stitch MCP (Tier 2). 5 rounds of iteration. Final selections: Card Popup (Muted Cyber), Intent Graph (Two-column timeline), Pet Window (Speech Bubbles).

gaps_noted: Boundary conditions for concurrency/failure not discussed
Boundary conditions for concurrent LLM calls need definition
Manual-input and settings pages not yet designed (UI)
